edges.description: Disease-associated variants interfere with the normal oxygen-sensing degradation machinery.
Early embryonic mosaic EPAS1 activation can include expanded developmental phenotypes such as neural tube defects.
Stabilized HIF-2alpha increases transcription of HIF target genes.
Pseudohypoxic signaling predisposes to PPGL-spectrum tumors and somatostatinoma.
HIF-2alpha target-gene upregulation includes persistent erythropoietin signaling, producing early childhood polycythemia.
Pseudohypoxic EPAS1/HIF-2alpha activation predisposes to multifocal paraganglioma.
The PPGL-spectrum tumor predisposition includes adrenal pheochromocytoma involvement.
EPAS1/HIF-2alpha activation predisposes to duodenal somatostatinoma in the syndrome triad.
Gallbladder disease occurs as an associated gastrointestinal manifestation in the somatostatinoma-containing syndrome spectrum.

meta.association: Early postzygotic somatic mosaic gain-of-function mutation

evidence.reference_title: New syndrome of paraganglioma and somatostatinoma associated with polycythemia.
evidence.snippet: Each patient carried an identical unique mutation in both types of tumors but not in germline DNA.

evidence.reference_title: Pacak-Zhuang syndrome: a model providing new insights into tumor syndromes.
evidence.snippet: Herein, we review our initial identification of a new syndrome of multiple paragangliomas, somatostatinomas, and polycythemia caused by early postzygotic mosaic mutations in EPAS1

nodes.description: Gallbladder disease is a frequent associated manifestation, particularly in patients with somatostatinoma, and includes chronic cholecystitis and cholelithiasis.

evidence.snippet: gallbladder disease, with four having chronic cholecystitis and two, cholelithiasis, usually in early adulthood at a median of 29 years (range 19–39).

nodes.description: Stabilized HIF-2alpha upregulates hypoxia-response genes including EPO, VEGFA, GLUT1, and END1, producing persistent erythrocytosis signals and a pseudohypoxic tumor program.

evidence.reference_title: New syndrome of paraganglioma and somatostatinoma associated with polycythemia.
evidence.snippet: An increase in the half-life of HIF2α was associated with upregulation of the hypoxia-related genes EPO, VEGFA, GLUT1, and END1 in tumors.

Polycythemia was detected in patients in early childhood (median 2 years, range from birth to 7 years). Erythropoietin (EPO) levels were an average of 5 times above the upper limit of normal in all patients.

nodes.description: Syndrome-associated HIF2A variants cluster near the oxygen-sensing proline residue, decrease prolyl hydroxylation, reduce VHL binding, and prolong HIF-2alpha half-life under normoxic conditions.

evidence.reference_title: New syndrome of paraganglioma and somatostatinoma associated with polycythemia.
evidence.snippet: The HIF2A mutations in these patients were clustered adjacent to an oxygen-sensing proline residue, affecting HIF2α interaction with the prolyl hydroxylase domain 2-containing protein, decreasing the hydroxylation of HIF2α, and reducing HIF2α affinity for the von Hippel-Lindau protein and its degradation.

nodes.description: Neural tube defects have been reported as an expanded developmental phenotype in Pacak-Zhuang syndrome, consistent with the early embryonic timing of the mosaic EPAS1 defect.

evidence.reference_title: Pacak-Zhuang syndrome: a model providing new insights into tumor syndromes.
evidence.snippet: our continued exploration of new disease phenotypes in this syndrome, including vascular malformations and neural tube defects.

nodes.description: HIF-2alpha pathway activation predisposes chromaffin and enteroendocrine lineages to recurrent multifocal neuroendocrine tumors within the pheochromocytoma/paraganglioma and somatostatinoma spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: New syndrome of paraganglioma and somatostatinoma associated with polycythemia.
evidence.snippet: Patients were found to have polycythemia, multiple PGLs, and duodenal somatostatinomas by imaging or biochemistry with somatic gain-of-function HIF2A mutations.

PGLs were found at a median age of 17 years (range 8-38) and SOMs at 29 years (range 22-38). PGLs were multiple, recurrent and metastatic in 100, 100 and 29% of all cases, and SOMs in 40, 40 and 60%, respectively.

nodes.description: Paragangliomas are the dominant PPGL-spectrum tumor manifestation in Pacak-Zhuang syndrome and are often multifocal, recurrent, and norepinephrine-producing.

evidence.snippet: PGL developed later in life at a median age of 17 years (range 8–38) in all patients. PGLs were predominantly of the norepinephrine-producing biochemical phenotype.

nodes.description: Adrenal involvement occurs in a substantial subset of patients, indicating a frequent pheochromocytoma component within the broader PPGL phenotype.

evidence.snippet: Patients were found to have multiple (100%), recurrent (100%), and metastatic (29%) PGLs, with adrenal involvement in four cases.

nodes.description: Polycythemia typically presents at birth or in early childhood and usually precedes tumor detection by years.

evidence.snippet: All patients were diagnosed with polycythemia before age 8

nodes.description: Early postzygotic gain-of-function mutations in EPAS1 create mosaic cell populations with constitutive HIF-2alpha activity.

evidence.reference_title: Pacak-Zhuang syndrome: a model providing new insights into tumor syndromes.
evidence.snippet: Herein, we review our initial identification of a new syndrome of multiple paragangliomas, somatostatinomas, and polycythemia caused by early postzygotic mosaic mutations in EPAS1

evidence.reference_title: New syndrome of paraganglioma and somatostatinoma associated with polycythemia.
evidence.snippet: Patients were found to have polycythemia, multiple PGLs, and duodenal somatostatinomas by imaging or biochemistry with somatic gain-of-function HIF2A mutations.

nodes.description: Somatostatinomas are usually duodenal, tend to appear after the PPGL phenotype, and may recur or metastasize despite young age of onset.

evidence.reference_title: New syndrome of paraganglioma and somatostatinoma associated with polycythemia.
evidence.snippet: Patients were found to have polycythemia, multiple PGLs, and duodenal somatostatinomas by imaging or biochemistry with somatic gain-of-function HIF2A mutations.
